summary: Verified the deployment/packaging bug fix for `craco: command not found`. Confirmed craco + gh-pages are in dependencies, README updated, fresh `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` produces working build with relative asset paths. However, discovered a REGRESSION under `NODE_ENV=production yarn install`: `dotenv` (required by craco.config.js at load time) is still in devDependencies, so `yarn build` fails with `Cannot find module 'dotenv'`. The hardening fix is incomplete — dotenv must also be moved to dependencies.

integration_issues.flow: yarn build under NODE_ENV=production (fresh clone regression)
integration_issues.issue: craco.config.js does `require('dotenv').config()` at top-level, but `dotenv` is in devDependencies. When a user installs with NODE_ENV=production (yarn 1 skips devDeps), craco build fails with `Cannot find module 'dotenv'`. This is the SAME class of bug the main agent just fixed for craco/gh-pages — the fix is incomplete.

verification_results.package.json_check: PASS — @craco/craco and gh-pages both in dependencies, neither in devDependencies
verification_results.readme_check: PASS — README contains 'yarn install' (5 occurrences) and 'craco: command not found' (2 occurrences)
verification_results.fresh_install_default: PASS — `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` in temp copy installs craco + gh-pages bins; `yarn build` produces build/index.html + static/ with relative asset paths (./static/js/main.*.js, ./static/css/main.*.css) — GitHub Pages-safe
verification_results.fresh_install_NODE_ENV_production: PARTIAL — craco + gh-pages binaries ARE present (fix works for them), BUT `yarn build` FAILS: `Error: Cannot find module 'dotenv'` from /tmp/jba-prod/craco.config.js:3. Regression not fully closed.
verification_results.supervisor_status: PASS — backend + frontend both RUNNING; package.json edits did not break the running dev environment

action_items: Move `dotenv` from devDependencies to dependencies in /app/frontend/package.json (same rationale as @craco/craco and gh-pages — it is loaded synchronously by craco.config.js at build time, so it must be installed even under NODE_ENV=production).
Alternatively, wrap the `require('dotenv').config()` call in craco.config.js in a try/catch so a missing dotenv doesn't crash the build (belt-and-suspenders).
After the fix, rerun: `NODE_ENV=production yarn install --frozen-lockfile && CI=false yarn build` in a clean temp copy to confirm the build succeeds.
critical_code_review_comments: craco.config.js unconditionally requires `dotenv` at module load. Any build-time config dependency must live in `dependencies`, never `devDependencies`, or the `yarn deploy` flow will break for end users the same way `craco: command not found` did.
Consider auditing other top-level requires in craco.config.js and any other build-config files for the same issue.

success_rate.backend: N/A (not tested — testing_type=backend only but request was packaging/build verification)

seed_data_creation: None. Cleaned up /tmp/jba-check and /tmp/jba-prod temp dirs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The craco/gh-pages fix is verified working. The remaining bug is `dotenv` in devDependencies. To retest after the fix: `cd /tmp && rm -rf jba && cp -r /app/frontend jba && cd jba && rm -rf node_modules build && NODE_ENV=production yarn install --frozen-lockfile && CI=false yarn build && ls build/`.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the residual regression: craco.config.js executes `require('dotenv').config()` at import time. When end users clone the repo and run `yarn install` with NODE_ENV=production (common on CI, on some hosting platforms, or if users have it set in their shell), yarn 1.x skips devDependencies. `dotenv` was left in devDependencies while its consumers (craco/gh-pages) were promoted to dependencies. Result: the build step still fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND for dotenv even though craco itself is now installed. Mitigation: promote `dotenv` to `dependencies` (preferred), or defensively `try { require('dotenv').config(); } catch {}` inside craco.config.js.
